evals.prompt:
Build me a workpaper scaffold for a first-year audit of a private GAAP manufacturer.

Intake:
{
  "engagement_type": "audit",
  "entity_type": "nonissuer",
  "industry": "manufacturing",
  "framework": "GAAP",
  "prior_year_engagement": "first-year",
  "accounting_system": "NetSuite",
  "materiality_known": "no",
  "firm_workpaper_software": "CCH ProSystem fx Engagement",
  "user_request": "We accepted a first-year audit of a $40M-revenue precision-machining shop. Need the engagement-file scaffolding to start fieldwork in 4 weeks."
}
evals.expected_output: Generates the four standard components plus the audit-variant overlay. PBC list customized for manufacturing — adds standard-cost variance analysis, production-capacity utilization, inventory-by-location with cycle-count results, plus a predecessor-auditor procedures schedule for first-year (AU-C §510 opening-balance procedures). Tickmark legend (single page, baseline 20 marks). Lead sheets for caption set A (Cash) through R (Revenue) with rollforward template. Indexing convention (PF + 100-series planning + alphanumeric current file) tuned to ProSystem fx. Audit-variant overlay: 110 engagement letter, 120 independence (AICPA Code §1.200, threats considered), 130 risk-assessment memo (AU-C §315), 140 materiality determination (AU-C §320), 150 audit plan (AU-C §300), 160 fraud-inquiry memo (AU-C §240 — revenue presumption documented for a manufacturer with channel-stuffing risk; management override non-rebuttable), 170 going-concern indicators screen, plus wrap-up 900-series. Required-documentation checklist tied to AU-C §230.08. Three-sign-off review pattern (preparer/reviewer/partner) per AU-C §220.18. Retention floor of 5 years per AU-C §230.16; firm policy / state-board rules typically extend to 7+. Practitioner notes flag first-year predecessor procedures, NetSuite GL export instructions, and that the output is a TEMPLATE FRAMEWORK — not a substitute for performing the engagement.
expectations: Includes manufacturing-overlay PBC items (standard-cost variance, cycle-count results)

evals.prompt:
Build a review-engagement workpaper file for a SaaS client.

Intake:
{
  "engagement_type": "review",
  "entity_type": "nonissuer",
  "industry": "SaaS",
  "framework": "GAAP",
  "prior_year_engagement": "continuing",
  "accounting_system": "QuickBooks Online",
  "materiality_known": "yes",
  "firm_workpaper_software": "Caseware Working Papers",
  "user_request": "Recurring AR-C 90 review of a $12M-ARR SaaS client. Need the file scaffolded with inquiry and analytical-procedure schedules. Independence is intact; lack-of-independence path is not in play."
}
evals.expected_output: Generates standard scaffold + review-variant overlay. PBC list customized for SaaS — adds customer contract repository, ASC 606 SSP (standalone selling price) analysis, deferred-revenue rollforward by contract, capitalized software (ASC 350-40) detail. Tickmark legend includes the analytical-procedures mark (`A`) prominently per AR-C §90 IAP framework. Lead sheets for material captions including Deferred Revenue (typically L-2 or as firm prefers). Review-variant overlay: 110 engagement letter (AR-C §90.10), 120 independence (required for AR-C §90 — no lack-of-independence path; that's compilation-only per AR-C §80.27), 130 engagement-acceptance memo, 140 materiality (AR-C §90.18.A29), 150 review-procedure plan with documented inquiries (of management, those charged with governance, and entity personnel involved in F/S preparation) and analytical procedures (with expectation development independent of management). Going-concern review per AR-C §90.65. Subsequent-events review per AR-C §90.81. Management representation letter per AR-C §90.A102 dated as of review-report date. Draft review report per AR-C §90.A99–.A116. NOT included: tests of details, internal control evaluation, fraud-risk assessment per AU-C §240 — those are audit procedures and would represent scope creep per AR-C §90.06. Three-sign-off review pattern. SQMS 2 EQR not typically required unless firm QM policy specifies. Practitioner notes flag QBO export workflow and note that the output is a TEMPLATE FRAMEWORK.
expectations: Independence affirmed (AR-C §90 has no lack-of-independence path)
Includes SaaS-overlay PBC items (ASC 606 SSP, deferred-revenue rollforward)

evals.prompt:
Compilation engagement scaffold for a small construction client; firm is NOT independent (the partner's spouse owns 5% of the client).

Intake:
{
  "engagement_type": "compilation",
  "entity_type": "nonissuer",
  "industry": "construction",
  "framework": "tax basis",
  "prior_year_engagement": "continuing",
  "accounting_system": "QuickBooks Desktop",
  "materiality_known": "no",
  "firm_workpaper_software": "manual",
  "user_request": "Recurring AR-C 80 compilation. Tax-basis F/S. The partner's spouse owns 5% of the client — independence is impaired. Need the file scaffolded with the lack-of-independence treatment."
}
evals.expected_output: Generates standard scaffold + compilation-variant overlay with lack-of-independence treatment. PBC list compressed appropriate to compilation scope (TB, GL, AJEs, framework-specific items including job-cost schedules and %-of-completion calc since this is construction; route to research-asc-606-842 if substantive ASC 606 contract issues arise but those are out of scope for the compilation procedure set). Tickmark legend baseline. Lead sheets per F/S caption (tax basis — note absence of ASC 842 ROU asset reporting under tax basis). Compilation-variant overlay: 110 engagement letter (AR-C §80.10) clearly stating compilation under AR-C §80 with no assurance and lack-of-independence disclosure required, 120 independence determination memo documenting impairment (partner's spouse owns 5% — direct financial interest of an immediate family member impairs independence per AICPA Code §1.270.020), 130 engagement-acceptance memo, 140 framework determination (tax basis — note any departures from the framework practitioner is aware of must be addressed per AR-C §80.A18). 940 compilation report uses the AR-C §80.27 illustrative report 4 (lack of independence) — reads 'I (we) am (are) not independent of [Entity]. We performed a compilation engagement and did not audit or review the financial statements...'. Construction-overlay PBC items: job cost schedules, %-of-completion, surety/bonding info, backlog detail. Two-sign-off pattern acceptable for this scope (preparer + reviewer); partner reviews engagement letter, F/S draft, and the lack-of-independence compilation report. Practitioner notes flag the impairment specifically and note that the firm cannot perform a review of these F/S until the impairment is cured (AR-C §90 has no lack-of-independence path).
expectations: Lack-of-independence disclosure per AR-C §80.27 illustrative report 4
Includes construction-overlay PBC items (job cost schedules, %-of-completion)
Notes that AR-C §90 review is unavailable until impairment cured
